title: Republic Services Weighs Acquisitions Against Free Cash Flow Headwinds
author: Chris Markoch
description: Republic Services reported Q4 2026 EPS above expectations but missed on revenue. The company guided for 3% YOY growth in 2026 revenue and EPS while announcing $1 billion in planned acquisitions. However, softer operating cash flow and potential free cash flow pressure from CapEx spending have raised investor concerns, causing the stock to decline despite the company's position as a defensive play in the waste management duopoly.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed earnings report with EPS beat but revenue miss. Forward guidance shows modest 3% growth. While the company benefits from defensive positioning and sector duopoly, concerns about free cash flow headwinds and reduced buyback capacity due to acquisition spending and CapEx weigh on sentiment. Stock showed bearish price action post-earnings.

Mentioned as part of the waste management duopoly with Republic Services. While the sector offers defensive characteristics and long-term contract revenue, the article notes WM has underperformed RSG over the last three years (19.6% vs 24.5% total return), suggesting neutral positioning without specific catalysts mentioned.

title: Textile Waste Management Market Report 2026-2035: A $13.87 Billion Market by 2030 with Veolia Environnement SA, Republic Services, TEXAID, and Infinited Fiber Co Leasing
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The textile waste management market is projected to grow from $9.25 billion in 2025 to $13.87 billion by 2030, driven by regulatory mandates, circular fashion investments, and advanced recycling technologies. Asia-Pacific leads the market while North America shows the fastest growth. Key innovations include enzymatic recycling and chemical recycling methods, with major players like Veolia Environnement SA, Republic Services, and Infinited Fiber Company leading the sector.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a key market leader in textile waste management with opportunities to capitalize on expanding recycling infrastructure investments and regulatory mandates.
Included as a major player in textile recycling, positioned to benefit from growing adoption of advanced recycling technologies and increased demand for sustainable fibers.
Listed as a major leading player in the textile waste management market, positioned to benefit from the projected 8.4% CAGR growth through 2030.

title: Packaging Waste Management Market Size, Trends, Segments, Share and Companies 2025-35
author: Towards Packaging
